scenario_results.reason: The lane progressed a normal hand-pay case using jackpot, patron, surveillance, and approval evidence rather than generic service notes.
Missing jackpot or fill artifacts correctly blocked action and produced an industry-specific evidence-gap exception.
Completion without signed floor, tax, or cage evidence was refused and held out of closure.
Cashless and wagering-account mismatches triggered dependency mapping and low-confidence handling instead of improvised patron resolution.
A progressive or fill variance was converted into rework and escalation rather than forced completion.
Requests for device, paytable, or game-rule authority were rejected and routed to technical or regulatory owners.
Suspicious cash or account behavior triggered correct cage or AML escalation without SAR-sensitive overreach.
The package used gaming-floor terminology and artifact shapes instead of generic hospitality or retail operations language.
Conflicting tribal, state, and tenant controls correctly triggered escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation preserved the public gaming baseline while separating tribal and commercial control paths.
Self-exclusion, underage, and patron-protection concerns stopped normal floor progression and routed to specialized owners.
Cross-system disagreement on jackpot or fill closure produced an unresolved conflict note instead of unsupported completion.

notes: All 12 scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated validation requirements are met with complete scenario and functionality coverage.
Runtime remains specification-based and no cage, AML, surveillance, tax, device-tech, or regulator authority is claimed.
